programme.title: Raanjhanaa
programme.desc: A small-town boy must bridge the class gap to win back childhood sweetheart.

programme.title: Makkhi
programme.desc: Sudeep, obsessed with Bindu, kills her lover Jani, who is reborn as a fly. Jani and Bindu team up to exact revenge on Sudeep in creative ways.

programme.title: Bol Radha Bol
programme.desc: Kishan's peaceful life is shattered when he returns home to find an imposter has taken over his identity, leading to a suspenseful journey to uncover the truth.

programme.title: Saaho
programme.desc: An undercover agent and his partner go after a thief who has stolen 2,000 crore rupees. Soon, they realise that the case is linked to the death of a crime lord and an emerging gang war.

programme.title: Laadla
programme.desc: Sheetal, a stubborn factory owner, marries union leader Raju to tame him, but ends up learning surprising lessons about power, love, and pride.

programme.title: Big Dhamaka
programme.desc: Trouble ensues when two lookalikes from varying backgrounds fall for the same girl. However, an unsuspecting connection between the duplicates further complicates their situation.

programme.title: Kanjoos Makhichoos
programme.desc: Man saves money for father's Char Dham Yatra dream, tragedy strikes in floods.

programme.title: Sonchiriya
programme.desc: After their leader's death, a notorious dacoit gang splits into factions. In their journey forward, they cross paths with Indumati and Khushi, sparking unexpected turns and emotional revelations.

programme.title: Machine
programme.desc: Sarah and Ransh share a common passion for car racing. When their friend Aditya decides to propose to Sarah, he meets with a car crash and dies.

programme.title: Jigarwala No.1
programme.desc: Dr Robin comes to India to recover his land from a gangster. He is persuaded by a writer who has a hidden agenda.

programme.title: Enemy
programme.desc: Rajiv grows envious of his childhood friend, Chozhan, as he is better than him at most things. The frenemies grow apart only to meet under very different circumstances several years later.

programme.title: Dhamaal
programme.desc: Four lazy friends-Roy, Manav, Aditya, and Boman-race to find hidden treasure in Goa. Their adventure turns risky when Inspector Kabir discovers their plan and begins pursuing them.

programme.title: Jawaan
programme.desc: Jai, an aspiring DRDO candidate, takes on his childhood friend Keshava, an international criminal, to protect India from a missile threat. A tale of patriotism and determination unfolds.

programme.title: Ramaiya Vastavaiya
programme.desc: City boy Ram falls for village girl Sona, raised by her protective brother Raghuveer. When Ram's mother insults Sona, Raghuveer challenges Ram to prove he deserves to marry her.

programme.title: Tumbbad
programme.desc: A revered goddess is angered when humans build a temple for her child, triggering divine punishment and revealing the consequences of mortal hubris and sacred boundaries.

programme.title: Tera Jadoo Chal Gayaa
programme.desc: To save her job, Pooja's friend tells their employer that she is engaged to Kabir, a known artist. However, this creates a misunderstanding between her and Raj, the man she actually loves.

programme.title: Wedding Pullav
programme.desc: Two best friends, Aditya and Anushka love each other but are unaware of their feelings. However, realisation strikes when they begin to feel jealous of each other's partners at Aditya's wedding.

programme.title: Hello Charlie
programme.desc: Charlie must transport a gorilla to Diu, unaware it's a man in costume. Chaos unfolds when they cross paths with a real gorilla, leading to a wild and unpredictable journey.

programme.title: Dhadak
programme.desc: Set in Rajasthan, this love story explores how the protagonists deal with issues like differences between castes and honour killings.

programme.title: Raksha Bandhan
programme.desc: Lala Kedarnath vows to marry off his four sisters before anything else, juggling family obligations and his chat shop, while putting his childhood crush, Sapna, on hold.

programme.title: Koi Mil Gaya
programme.desc: An autistic young man's life changes dramatically after meeting an alien, sparking unexpected friendship, self-discovery, and new perspectives on communication, identity, and belonging.

programme.title: Real Tevar
programme.desc: Story about a rich guy, who believes in doing what his heart says. Though he has everything in life, there is something else he desires to accomplish.

programme.title: Skanda
programme.desc: A young man renowned for his martial arts skills sees his life take a dramatic turn when a secret government agency recruits him for a dangerous mission to thwart the plans of a group of terrorists.

programme.title: IPC 376
programme.desc: A police officer delves into a mysterious informant's ability to foresee murders, uncovering a sinister mystery that reveals a shocking truth.

programme.title: Aliens 2042
programme.desc: Earth unites against alien water thieves in 2042, except China. Will humanity repel the invaders or lose control to aliens? suspenseful global showdown awaits.

programme.title: English Vinglish
programme.desc: Mom faces family disrespect due to language barrier, highlighting challenges of quiet strength and resilience.

programme.title: Jersey

programme.title: Judgementall Hai Kya

programme.desc: A hard-hitting crime drama where an upright cop battles corruption and criminal politics, seeking justice while confronting brutal realities of the rural police system.

programme.title: Daraar
programme.desc: Wealthy Raj Malhotra lives with his mother. He meets Priya Bhatia, who works with a real estate agency and falls in love with her.

programme.title: Naseeb
programme.desc: Pooja's father rejects her love for humble Krishna. Determined, Krishna leaves to become rich, but when he returns, he is heartbroken to find Pooja already married. Fate takes an unexpected turn.

programme.title: A Flying Jatt
programme.desc: Aman, a martial arts teacher, discovers superpowers during a fight with Raka. But toxic waste strengthens Raka, forcing Aman into a fierce battle to defeat him.

programme.title: Bloody Mary
programme.desc: In an attempt to fulfill their dreams, three friends get entangled in trouble related to a director's death. Meanwhile, they confront an inspector while trying to save themselves.

programme.title: Daav Pench
programme.desc: Mahendran borrows a huge amount of money for his daughter's operation. However, when a group of criminals steals his money, Mahendran seeks police inspector Tamilarasan's help.

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Were the ancient Scottish tribes too much for the Roman Empire? Or was Scotland simply not worth conquering? Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter looks back on three centuries of contact and conflict with Scotland’s Roman invaders. The first Tay Bridge, the first depiction of tartan and forgotten Roman camps that once held thirty-five thousand men. A story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords, and one with fascinating modern parallels.

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how an international team of scientists is trying to change people's perceptions of sharks. Their research is revealing that sharks can be sociable and intelligent, and they could even help solve some of the toughest medical challenges of the 21st century. However, the breakthroughs come at a time when many sharks face extinction. Science may now be the only way to save them.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 April 1987, featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde & Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn & Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent D'Arby.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 April 1990 and featuring Sonia, Faith No More and Adamski.

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A deep dive into the BBC’s archives to celebrate the great Roy Orbison – full-time rock 'n’ roll legend, part-time Traveling Wilbury and the man Elvis famously described as ‘the best singer in the world’. The Big O’s story was in many ways a sad one, full of family tragedies and heartbreak, and his extraordinary voice captured all those emotions and more, bringing a new dimension to popular music. This programme features some of the hits that had fans enraptured and fellow music superstars singing his praises, with performances from his many visits to the UK including Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh Pretty Woman.

programme.title: Roy Orbison and Friends: A Black and White Night
programme.desc: This TV concert classic features Roy Orbison performing his greatest hits with friends Bruce Springsteen, Tom Waits, Elvis Costello, Jackson Browne, kd lang, Jennifer Warnes and Bonnie Raitt. The TCB Band, which backs all the featured artists, was Elvis Presley's band until his death in 1977 and includes James Burton, Glen D Hardin, Jerry Scheff and Ronnie Tutt, with musical director T Bone Burnett. Filmed at the Coconut Grove nightclub in Los Angeles, in black and white (hence the title!), the show was first broadcast on HBO in 1988, the year of Roy Orbison's death.

programme.title: Roy Sings Orbison
programme.desc: Roy Orbison sings some of his greatest hits, including Only the Lonely, Crying, Penny Arcade, Blue Bayou, Running Scared, Candy Man, In Dreams, Mean Woman Blues, It's Over and Oh, Pretty Woman.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 April 1987, featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde & Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn & Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent D'Arby.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 April 1990 and featuring Sonia, Faith No More and Adamski.

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A deep dive into the BBC’s archives to celebrate the great Roy Orbison – full-time rock 'n’ roll legend, part-time Traveling Wilbury and the man Elvis famously described as ‘the best singer in the world’. The Big O’s story was in many ways a sad one, full of family tragedies and heartbreak, and his extraordinary voice captured all those emotions and more, bringing a new dimension to popular music. This programme features some of the hits that had fans enraptured and fellow music superstars singing his praises, with performances from his many visits to the UK including Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh Pretty Woman.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life of snooker legend Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor and Barry Hearn.

programme.title: Gold Run
programme.desc: April 9, 1940. The German war machine occupies Norway. One of their primary objectives is to seize the nation’s entire gold reserves. But thanks to a few courageous individuals, the gold is smuggled out - just as the Germans come.​​ ​​This is the incredible true story of how 55 tonnes of gold was transported through rough winter landscapes on trucks, trains and boats​ by a group of unlikely resistance fighters ahead of the invading Nazi forces.​

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Escapologist Harry Houdini was one of the icons of the early 20th century. Restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists recall his extraordinary impact on audiences and reveal the secrets behind some of his most amazing escapes.

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Professor Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary periods in British history: the Industrial Revolution. He explains the unique economic, social and political conditions that by the 19th century, led to Britain becoming the richest, most powerful nation on Earth. It was a time that transformed the way people think, work and play forever. He traces the unprecedented explosion of new ideas and technological inventions that transformed Britain's agricultural society into an increasingly industrial and urbanised one. The documentary explores two fascinating questions - why did the industrial revolution happen when it did, and why did it happen in Britain? Professor Black discusses the reasons behind this transformation - from Britain's coal reserves, which gave it a seemingly inexhaustible source of power, to the ascendancy of political liberalism, with engineers and industrialists able to meet and share ideas and inventions. He explains the influence that geniuses like Josiah Wedgewood had on the consumer revolution and travels to Antigua to examine the impact Britain's empire had on this extraordinary period of growth.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life of snooker legend Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor and Barry Hearn.

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: River takes its audience on a journey through space and time spanning six continents, showing rivers on a scale and from perspectives never seen before.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores how modern art and the Riviera grew up together when France's Cote d'Azur became the hedonistic playground and experimental studio for the great masters of 20th-century painting. With Henri Matisse and Pablo Picasso resident on the coast, other artists from Jean Cocteau to Henri Lartigue, Raoul Dufy to Fernand Leger and Francis Picabia to Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. As transatlantic liners brought America's super-rich to the region, art and celebrity became integrally intertwined as cultural gurus and multimillionaires all partied on the beach. In an era of sunshine and bathing, of cinema and fast cars, of the Ballets Russes and Monte Carlo casinos, Grant discovers the extraordinary output of what became briefly the world's creative hub.

programme.title: Gershwin's Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: An investigation into Gershwin's classic composition Summertime, which has been recorded in almost every style of music - from jazz to opera, rock to reggae, soul to samba.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Artemis II mission has been presented as a major milestone in human spaceflight, with global audiences following live coverage and access provided by NASA. Questions have been raised about how official feeds, astronaut storytelling and media partnerships shaped reporting. Rebecca Morelle, BBC News science editor, describes the reporting environment she experienced. The BBC has announced plans to cut around 2,000 jobs and save £500 million, citing financial pressures including declining licence fee income and rising production costs. The changes come ahead of charter renewal negotiations and increased competition. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC’s acting director-general, outlines the scale of the challenge. A Kuwaiti-American journalist is reportedly being held by authorities in Kuwait after posting footage of a US Air Force jet crash near Kuwait City. The case has drawn attention to press freedom in the region. Jodie Ginsberg, CEO of the Committee to Protect Journalists, sets out the known details.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo visits the cool of Sweden's capital city to continue her culinary journey. Rachel visits one of Stockholm's hippest restaurants, where rump of deer is on the menu. Back home, she's inspired by Sweden's traditional cinnamon buns and bakes her own savoury Swedish buns as well as cooking venison steak with celeriac puree, pickled blackberries and carrot petals and a lingonberry and raspberry marzipan tart, inspired by the Swedish lingonberry.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims, giving them fake cover stories. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Rob Bell heads to North America's largest ski resort as it fires up its lifts for its 60th season. Whistler Blackcomb has become a bucket list destination for skiers, so Rob - who used to work as a 'liftie' there in his youth - goes behind the scenes to find out what goes into such a mammoth operation. He gets back to work on the lifts, hears the indigenous reaction to having a giant of tourism arrive on their land and spends time with the ski patrollers working to keep the customers safe.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: For Talking Movies presenter Tom Brook, April 2026 marks a momentous milestone. It will be the 50th anniversary of Tom joining the BBC as a trainee news journalist. Over the decades, from London, Berlin and mostly New York, he has reported on culture and cinema in thousands of stories and programmes broadcast on the BBC. Tom has interviewed countless leading figures in cinema, from early pioneers like Bette Davis and Jimmy Cagney up to contemporary star names, including Timothee Chalamet, Meryl Streep and Lady Gaga. Tom has also met US presidents and UK prime ministers, and leaders in global culture from Bollywood to LA. In a special edition of Talking Movies, he looks back at his career and the actors and filmmakers he has encountered over 50 years. He reflects on how the film industry has changed over the last five decades and how, in very tumultuous times, he believes cinema is more relevant and useful to our lives than ever before.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, BBC Eye follows Kenyans at the centre of a groundbreaking legal battle to track down their British soldier fathers using novel DNA methods. And more than seven weeks on from US and Israeli strikes on Iran, Selin Girit reports from Turkey on the country’s reaction to a widening regional conflict.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: In a case of mistaken identity, a man is stabbed to death. An intelligence analyst helps convict the killers, and a trace evidence expert helps find the weapon used to kill a teenager.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Some 1960s newspapers are uncovered by Martin in Hampshire, Martel is impressed with what’s on offer in Yorkshire, and Tommy feels at home in east London.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A football match between British and German troops on Christmas Day in 1914 was one of the most enduring and symbolic moments of the First World War. In this touching episode, Danny Sebastian hears how a young goalkeeper from Newark took part in that iconic match and may have provided the match ball – a gift from his brother back home. Kicking off their hunt for three profitable antiques are friends and aviation engineers Hannah and Shelly, who reckon they will be shopping to the last minute. Their opponents in blue are dad and daughter Paul and Lorraine, who predict that there will be tears if they don’t win. Straight after the whistle, the reds spot a set of military style storage boxes and a collection of sweetheart brooches, but expert Roo Irvine doesn’t think either will woo the bidders. Meanwhile, the blues are reflecting on an antique hand mirror and some mis-matched metal armour, which is going for an arm and a leg. In Derby, auctioneer James Lewis tries to hammer out profits, and for both teams, there are shiny bonus buys to tempt them.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Brenda finds love at the home of a clock collector, but has she fallen for a killer?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams is with a couple from London in the market for a new home in the countryside of Oxfordshire or Wiltshire that has enough space for them and their two young sons. Taking time away from house hunting, Briony learns why the relatively new practice of not mowing the grass in the month of May is here to stay.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Looking to change their lives, with the help of Dom and his team of mentors, are woodworker Neil and embroiderer Catherine. After retiring, former police officer Neil wants to build a successful woodworking business, but so far, the orders haven’t been coming in. Mentor Bruce thinks he’s doing too much and needs to create a core range to have a chance at success. When her father passed away, Catherine decided to make her embroidery business dreams a reality, but she hasn’t been able to earn a living from it. Her mentor Stephanie thinks she has great potential but is spreading herself too thin and needs to streamline her work. Both are given eight weeks to follow their mentors’ advice, but can they create successful, sustainable businesses?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Justin, Dean, Serena, Joe and Andrea – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Labour
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Labour Party for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne and Angellica Bell bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: On her very first day running her local Post Office, Seema discovered a cash shortfall at the till. She had to put in her own money to make the books add up. Every day, more and more money appeared to be missing. She didn't know it then, but she was the latest victim of the infamous Post Office Horizon accounting scandal, which saw hundreds of sub-postmasters and sub-postmistresses falsely convicted of theft, fraud and false accounting, based on faulty computer data. Desperate to keep her business afloat, Seema even sold her gold wedding ring in an attempt to meet the shortfall. Following a trial, she was convicted and sent to prison whilst pregnant. Seema’s story puts a devastating human face on the Post Office scandal, one of the gravest miscarriages of justice in modern British history. Bill Bailey pairs Seema with Jack, an artist of great emotional sensitivity, whose richly layered paintings explore, amongst other things, resilience. Will Jack's approach to Seema's portrait offer the perfect way to tell her story, full of friendships, family and strength?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: An off-grid community is thrust into the spotlight when a land developer is terrorised by the image of a Green Man. As tensions rise, secrets surface that someone wants buried.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts, with guest panellists comedian Finlay Christie and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: It’s time for Conor and Jock to try the exciting Cork dating scene when two girls visiting the city catch their attention.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia looks back at some of the highlights from the series, featuring her studio audience and an array of star-studded sofa guests, including Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler, Jamie Dornan and many more.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: 2257. A colony world community without women, where thoughts are heard aloud, is disrupted when teen Todd finds crash survivor Viola, and the pair must flee ruthless Mayor Prentiss.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Georgina Hayden and Richard Bainbridge, and by special guests Nish Kumar and the Great British Menu winner! Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Chris explores the real Wrexham, from historic lager and five-generation fruit farms to vegan pioneers, Portuguese flavours and a fire-cooked feast celebrating local produce.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: It’s FA Cup semi-final weekend. We preview Manchester City v Southampton and Chelsea v Leeds. Wayne Rooney meets Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes, and we look ahead to a big weekend at the bottom of the Premier League table and Arsenal’s Women’s Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: This episode takes place in Shepton Mallet, Somerset, with Natasha Raskin Sharp at the helm, and the reds and blues led by John Cameron and Stephanie Connell. Which team will make the biggest profit or the smallest loss at auction? Meanwhile, Natasha tries her hand at making a famous local delicacy.

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan is at the Running Show to look ahead to the 46th London Marathon, which promises to set a new world record for the most participants in a marathon. She meets some of the inspirational charity runners, all with their own unique reasons to run this year, including those who are lacing up their trainers to raise millions of pounds for brilliant causes. And she is joined by former marathon world record holder Paula Radcliffe to look ahead to some of the most competitive elite races ever run.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 8 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Live coverage from Wembley as Southampton take on Manchester City in the FA Cup semi-final. It’s 50 years since Southampton beat Manchester United to cause one of the biggest cup final upsets in history, and the Championship team produced another shock in the quarter-finals this year, when they knocked out Premier League leaders Arsenal. Manchester City have reached the semi-final stage for a record eighth consecutive year. Last year, they made it through to the final only to be beaten by Crystal Palace.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Fred Sirieix, Laura Whitmore, Eddie Kadi, Josh Widdicombe, Vanessa Feltz and Scarlett Moffatt.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is at the helm of one of the world’s most iconic quizzes. Eight celebrities are tested on their general knowledge as they compete to win up to £50,000 for their chosen charities. Round by round, one player will be voted the weakest link by their fellow contestants and will leave with nothing. Who will be crowned the strongest link and take home the prize for their charity? The celebrity contestants in this episode are Aisling Bea, La Voix, Ben Bailey Smith, Anna Richardson, Samantha Kinghorn, Will Best, Jason Byrne and Jacqui Joseph.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Our heroes step up as a mystery virus sweeps across Holby.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee’s favourite band is Oasis, which is why he rushed online to buy tickets to see them in concert. A year on, he still can’t come to terms with the fact that he lost his place in the queue and missed out. Lucy suggests a night in watching Oasis on the TV instead, but Lee can’t get over this feeling he’s had for a long time... that it was all Lucy’s fault.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of six Premier League games including Arsenal against Newcastle, with Mikel Arteta’s side looking to return to the top of the table after being replaced by Manchester City in midweek. Tottenham face a crucial clash with Wolves at Molineux, while West Ham take on Everton at London Stadium. Fulham host Aston Villa, and Crystal Palace go to Anfield. There are also highlights of Sunderland versus Nottingham Forest at the Stadium of Light from Friday night.

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.desc: Wayne Rooney catches up with Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes.

programme.title: Candyman
programme.desc: For decades, residents of Chicago’s Cabrini-Green have heard stories of a supernatural killer known only as Candyman, allegedly the resurrected soul of a murdered slave wanting to exact vengeance on his killers. In the present day, visual artist Anthony McCoy (Yahya Abdul-Mateen II) and his girlfriend (Teyonah Parris) have moved into a luxury loft condo in a now gentrified Cabrini-Green. A chance encounter with a local old-timer (Colman Domingo) exposes Anthony to the true story behind Candyman and unleashes a wave of violence that puts him on a collision course with destiny.

programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of six Premier League games including Arsenal against Newcastle, with Mikel Arteta’s side looking to return to the top of the table after being replaced by Manchester City in midweek. Tottenham face a crucial clash with Wolves at Molineux, while West Ham take on Everton at London Stadium. Fulham host Aston Villa, and Crystal Palace go to Anfield. programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Fifty-nine thousand charity runners take to the streets of the capital for the 46th London Marathon, which aims to set a new world record for the most participants in a marathon. They follow the fastest elite men, women and wheelchair racers in the world, running on the same roads and cheered on by the same enthusiastic crowds. Gabby Logan leads the coverage throughout the day, and she is joined by a team of reporters spread across the 26.2-mile course, including JJ Chalmers, Blue Peter’s Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey. They are on hand to tell the inspirational stories of ordinary people doing extraordinary things and raising millions of pounds for some great causes.

programme.title: BBC Weather

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild microclimate have long made it one of Britain’s most productive growing regions. Early spring brings the so-called hungry gap, but the vale has a long history of producing food, even in these leaner months. From the first spears of its world-famous asparagus to high-tech growers producing food year round, and from historic market gardens to traditional orchards alive with early pollinators, Adam and Sammi explore a landscape shaped by both tradition and innovation. While fresh produce may be scarce elsewhere, the Vale of Evesham is already beginning to flourish. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how tawny owls, badgers, dormice and bumblebees have made a home in a beautiful woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow returns to Beaumaris Castle in Anglesey, north Wales, where finds include Fabergé jewels, a Titanic mystery and works by local artist Charles Tunnicliffe.

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: When Matty and Leanne catch each other stealing from their casino, they agree to stop. But as their lives spiral out of control, the pact they have made frays.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London

programme.title: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents highlights from Wembley of Sunday’s FA Cup semi-final between Chelsea and Leeds United. Leeds reached this stage of the competition for the first time in 39 years after beating West Ham on penalties, while Chelsea thrashed League One side Port Vale 7-0 in their quarter-final. The programme also features highlights of Saturday’s semi-final between Manchester City and Southampton.

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: The big names and the big games from the Women’s Super League, with highlights and analysis of all the action.

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show

programme.title: Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to prove that cars can be art by taking over an entire modern art gallery in Middlesbrough and filling it with motoring-related works, including some remarkable creations of their own. In their quest to prove that cars can be more popular than traditional art, the presenters set themselves the challenge of attracting more visitors to their exhibition than will visit a more traditional gallery over the same period, a task that forces them to engage in some unusual promotional activities. Also on the show, Jeremy is on the test track in a new British supercar, the sensationally fast and expensive Noble M600.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey tackles the subject of death by immersing herself in the world of undertaking at a century-old funeral director's.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve gets strangled by a python on a night-time river expedition, climbs a 40-metre tree and finds out why our thirst for palm oil is threatening bears and orangutans.

programme.title: Despicable Me
programme.desc: Delighting in all things wicked, supervillain Gru hatches a plot to steal the moon. Surrounded by an army of little yellow Minions, he has to incorporate three orphans into his evil plans.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: After an island-wide blackout causes havoc in Honoré, the team trace the source to a local substation and discover an electrocuted dead body. On first appearances, it seems like a tragic accident, but when Neville and the team investigate the computer repair shop where the victim worked, they discover a hidden world of data mining and crypto currency. It turns out the victim was stealing electricity in order to mine thousands and thousands in crypto. With so much money at stake, it soon becomes clear that this was no accidental death after all. The only problem is that all three suspects were together at the time of the victim’s electrocution. So if it was murder, how did they do it?

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Disarming Welsh DS Janie Mallowan is desperate to solve some big cases and progress her career. While investigating the death by apparent suicide of wealthy property developer Carwyn Rees, she meets none other than John Chapel, the retired actor who played her TV detective idol, Inspector Charles Caesar. Eccentric and reclusive John uses his insight into character to deduce that Carwyn was murdered, though Janie is initially sceptical of his conclusions. When forensics prove his theory right, Janie quietly asks him to help with the case. Having shut himself away from the world for years, John is initially reluctant to help. But together they work to solve an intriguing mystery involving an environmental activist, a grieving widow and a bitter business rival, painting the portrait of a man who kept a lot of secrets from those closest to him. And although John and Janie are not ready to admit it, this first case is the start of an iconic crime-solving partnership, and a friendship, too.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: After a walker is found dead on a Welsh mountain trail, John’s insight into the unidentified woman quickly turns this into a murder investigation. Janie ropes John into joining her mam Yvonne’s walking group to help solve the case and crack the identity of this mysterious woman. John is reluctant to go out and socialise, but he can’t resist the call of another mystery. John and Janie join forces to solve two mysteries: the identity of the victim and a cunning murder plot with a large dose of blackmail and revenge. Janie’s fierce defence of one of the suspects reveals more about her past, while John finds himself out of the house and enjoying himself a lot more than he’s ready to admit...

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's friends and family gather round when his boyfriend dumps him on the doorstep. Can he fight for his love and win back his man?

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Sick of dating creeps, Lucinda is given the royal princess treatment courtesy of her fairy godmother. Sal auditions for a talent show, and Dickie offers sage advice in the workplace.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, from Northern Irish creator rarewitch. The cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical, and Gambo forgets how to breathe. Surreal, chaotic and hilariously absurd – welcome to the madcap world of Glue.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service has decided to take the women’s rape cases to trial. But with court delays and barrister strikes, will they ever get their day in court?

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Facing court delays and the anxiety of preparing for court, Stacey follows the young women, who question if they can continue. Will they get the outcome they are hoping for?

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain’s most maligned ethnic minorities.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Orbiting a quiet backwater planet, the massed forces of the universe's deadliest species gather, drawn to a mysterious message that echoes out to the stars. And amongst them, the Doctor. Rescuing Clara from a family Christmas dinner, the Time Lord and his best friend must learn what this enigmatic signal means for his own fate and that of the universe.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A festive trip down memory lane prompts Pat to question his very identity. The ghosts come up with a surprise Christmas present for Alison, whose family lunch does not go to plan.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It’s coming up to Christmas, and Mike’s mum is staying. Betty wants to be helpful, but it isn’t long before she is driving Mike and Alison round the bend. Betty has overstayed her welcome, but Alison and Mike don’t quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong at Button House. She adopts extreme measures to make the house safe and in doing so endangers the resident Ghosts – will they come out of this unscathed?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isn't your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called 'sir' once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie, who manages Miranda's joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink, she gets overexcited by the prospect of her first real date and decides it's time to try to be more girly. She goes shopping for a new outfit to impress Gary, but her makeover doesn't go quite to plan.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so he'll see her in a sexual light. Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love. She goes, only to discover it's run by her old school teacher Mr Clayton, who Stevie then starts dating, much to Miranda's disgust. Miranda then thinks that a tango class would be more romantic for her and Gary, but again things don't quite go to plan.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad, Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum, Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than he'd originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Ken’s house of all his junk – or as Ken calls it, ‘his memories’. As Ken’s suspicions over Richard's true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob, local handyman and Richard’s former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Concerned that Ken is spending too much time on his own, Richard offers to take him to the local golf club, unaware that he’s about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than he'd originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists – and a stubborn Ken – in order to secure Ken a doctor’s appointment on the same day that he is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and embarks on a journey to track down the elusive Brian – Ken’s oldest friend, with whom he lost touch with years ago – in order to invite him to Ken’s surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jean’s ashes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny is reeling after being chatted up by Louise's dad. He decides to become more macho, but his plan backfires, with shocking consequences. Louise is feeling let down by her dad and becomes an alcoholic. And Donna refuses to have Gaz's baby, leaving him only one option for getting the child he longs for.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Sitcom about the lives and loves of five twentysomethings in Runcorn. Janet is reeling that Gaz tried to take Corinthian and decides to increase security in the home. Jonny gets a job as a farmer. Donna persuades Gaz to try tantric sex, but he gets hooked on it, much to her frustration. Louise wants a new family, and she knows exactly where to find one.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya tries to help Ravi, Nugget stands up for himself, and Lauren explores a new business venture.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together, Mark tries to help Lauren, and Elaine and Ian attend a gala.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki starts to worry about the family, Max and Cindy grow closer, and the Mitchells rally together.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max and Cindy share a moment, Ravi finally gets the help he needs, Lauren and Mark team up, and Sam gives Mark an idea.

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
programme.desc: Over the course of five social occasions, a committed bachelor must consider the notion that he may have discovered love.

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Documentary investigating events leading up to the shocking death of Friends star Matthew Perry and the woman at the centre of the case, dubbed the 'Ketamine Queen' by US prosecutors. Jasveen Sangha, a drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen, has pleaded guilty to five federal charges in Los Angeles, including one count of distributing ketamine resulting in death or bodily injury. She faces up to 65 years in prison for supplying Perry with the drugs that led to his death. Sangha was one of five people charged in the case. Prosecutors described her home as a 'drug-selling emporium', where authorities found approximately 79 vials of ketamine and other drugs, including methamphetamine, cocaine and Xanax. Presented by Amber Haque, the film features exclusive, first-on-the-record interviews with Sangha's friends and examines her role in Perry’s death and Hollywood’s ketamine drug network.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Spending time with people who regularly steal from shops, Stacey gets to grips with the shoplifting epidemic and what it’s like for the frontline staff trying to prevent thefts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Eight regional champions cook their main courses again in a bid to secure a place at a banquet celebrating the British film industry. The multi-award-winning director of such films as The Queen, Philomena, My Beautiful Laundrette and Dangerous Liaisons, Sir Stephen Frears, joins Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight main courses. And in a twist, a mystery wildcard chef is invited into the kitchen to enter the fray. Can they steal the winning crown from the other chefs?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the dessert final, the last chance for the chefs to win a dish at a red-carpet banquet in Liverpool to celebrate the British film industry. Michelin-starred chefs Tom Kerridge and Lorna McNee and comedian Phil Wang join GBM royalty Dame Prue Leith on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight fantastic dessert courses.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: In August 1991, Michael signs the most lucrative deal in music history with Sony Music and prepares for what his team believes will be a landmark year. Then, a 13-year-old boy accuses Michael of child sexual abuse. Detectives describe the shock of investigating the world’s most famous performer, while inside Michael’s camp, aides recall disbelief and a frantic effort to keep Michael’s worldwide tour, business empire and public image intact. As the story breaks, police search Michael’s home, and the media storm intensifies. Michael publicly denies the allegations, condemning what he calls a 'trial by media'.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After coming to Dom’s business boot camp in the summer of 2023, stone carver Louise from Oxfordshire and stained-glass artist Charlotte from Bristol built dream businesses. Louise arrived at the boot camp, looking for help to make stone carving her full-time job. Now, Dom visits her studio to find out how she has done exactly that and how much money she makes from it. Charlotte reveals how she has built a sustainable stained-glass business after giving up her job in a vet’s practice and how much she now earns from her work.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay, but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it’s Ronan’s next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling – the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Containing a neurotoxin with no known antidote, only highly trained chefs can prepare the fish. Will Michael give it a try? Travelling eastwards along the Honshu coast, Michael visits Hitachi’s Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured. He is thrilled to get inside the factory and see these amazing machines up close. To end this leg, Michael arrives by Shinkansen into Hiroshima, the city devastated by the atomic bomb dropped by US forces on 6th August 1945. Michael crosses the city to visit the Peace Memorial Park, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search, with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster, to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing. Ivydene garden near Comber is spread across three acres. Owners Robert and Michael have three separate areas of planting - a formal layout at the front of their home, a secret garden at the side, and at the back, a wildflower meadow and woodland area. In total contrast, there’s a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, where owner George has sourced unusual plants from all around the world. Finally, the judges view a rock garden in Ballynahinch. A large expanse of native rock dominates this four-acre garden, created by passionate owner Brigid. Diarmuid, Carol and Penny are searching for a garden which the owners have poured their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide is worthy of a place in the Greatest Garden final?

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There is a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will bear fruit later in the year. Ruth is focusing on growing for those with a small space, returning to the vertical garden that she started last year. Vertical gardening uses structures that allow plants to be stacked upwards - ideal for anyone with just a small plot or patio. She also shows how this method can still be very productive. George is in his beloved fruit cage, where after a clear-out last year, he plants a bed of new raspberry varieties and propagates new growth from Beechgrove’s existing fig tree. There is a Back to Basics guide from Colin Crosbie’s plot just outside Dumfries, and a review of Beechgrove’s gravel and grass garden, which will be thinned and replanted over the course of this season.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the garden explodes with spring colour, Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. He also plants in the woodland garden and pots up summer bulbs. Sue Kent is in her vibrant Swansea garden, sowing tender crops, planting echiums and harvesting a delicious salad bowl. Jamie Butterworth is in Essex to discover why the humble euphorbia deserves to be considered one of the brightest stars of the border. There’s also a trip to West Sussex to visit renowned garden designer Rosemary Alexander in her private garden. Plus a passionate anemone grower preparing for their debut at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George’s Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more. The chefs need to work together to wow the guests with their depth of flavour and flawless presentation. Who will triumph and become 2026's champion of champions?

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman introduces Suzi Ruffell and Loyiso Gola to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: Don't Look Now
programme.desc: Grieving the loss of their young daughter in a tragic accident, Laura and John Baxter visit Venice, where John is helping to restore a dilapidated church. As John works, Laura is befriended by two sisters, one of whom warns them to leave the city, claiming she has seen their dead daughter. Classic mystery thriller from Nicolas Roeg.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the seventh day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reports of antisemitic incidents have risen in the UK over the last few years, leaving many British Jews feeling fearful and under threat. In October 2025, a knifeman tried to enter Heaton Park synagogue - two members of the congregation were killed. Judith Moritz examines how abuse, intimidation and violence aimed at Jewish communities has become more widespread, hearing from the government’s independent reviewer of terror legislation who believes the normalisation of antisemitism makes violence more likely.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George’s Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more. The chefs need to work together to wow the guests with their depth of flavour and flawless presentation. Who will triumph and become 2026's champion of champions?

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, BBC Eye follows Kenyans at the centre of a groundbreaking legal battle to track down their British soldier fathers using novel DNA methods. And more than seven weeks on from US and Israeli strikes on Iran, Selin Girit reports from Turkey on the country’s reaction to a widening regional conflict.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Millie the Squid devises a set of tough penguin games to test the Piripenguins, Pancake and Nugget must stick together and rely on their friendship to succeed.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A triple solar eclipse is about to happen! Cosima and Big Lizard race down the volcano and hitch a lift on a dragonfly so that they can get vital equipment to Dad in the nick of time.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Chipmunk accidentally gets hit by a ball during a game of baseball. To make him feel better, the ducklings feed him popcorn. The Chipmunk pretends to be sick to get more and more privileges. Weasy has had enough!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are made to clean the Cave of Mirrors as punishment for frolicking before a quest is complete, and they accidentally release the Party Harpy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse’s light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Life is back to normal for Roy and friends, until wild Pokémon begin to act strangely...

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe become real-life pop stars, with dance moves provided by The Next Step cast.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Legend, Cyclone, Nitro and Sabre return to compete at a hotel check-in. With a hidden earpiece, will they obey all silly instructions for the chance to win or tap out and risk losing?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Join Joel, Abby and Shini on an epic Northern Ireland adventure! Joel finds out how potatoes are turned into crisps and invents his very own brand new flavour! Shini learns how to care for horses before saddling up for a ride along the stunning coastline, and we find out who is crowned the ultimate Celtic warrior.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of bat-hunting boas, meets a team working to save the deadly but cute slow loris, and he comes face to face with the king of snakes: the king cobra.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the garden explodes with spring colour, Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. He also plants in the woodland garden and pots up summer bulbs. Sue Kent is in her vibrant Swansea garden, sowing tender crops, planting echiums and harvesting a delicious salad bowl. Jamie Butterworth is in Essex to discover why the humble euphorbia deserves to be considered one of the brightest stars of the border. There’s also a trip to West Sussex to visit renowned garden designer Rosemary Alexander in her private garden. Plus a passionate anemone grower preparing for their debut at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 8 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary. How does a chimpanzee see the world? A research project at Edinburgh Zoo is designed to answer just that question in an innovative new way - by training chimps to use video touch screens and giving them a special chimp-proof camera. How will they react to tools which in evolutionary terms are a few million years ahead of them? As chimp specialist Betsy Herrelko finds out, trying to communicate with chimps using video technology has its trials and tribulations as power struggles, bites and fights get in the way of the hairy chimp directors. However, by the end of the programme we are privileged to see the world's first film shot by chimpanzees.

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Rugby
programme.desc: Live coverage as Wales take on England in the Women's Six Nations.

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest news, analysis, reaction and interviews from the afternoon’s biggest football matches.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This edition of Flog It! comes from Longleat in Wiltshire, home to the world-famous safari park and 16th-century Longleat House. In this rather special show, Paul Martin is joined by experts Michael Baggott and Claire Rawle. Whilst picking out a selection of interesting antiques and collectables to be sold at a local auction, Claire spots an unusual Tibetan teapot. This leads to the momentous discovery of other of Tibetan artefacts belonging to the same owner, a collection of controversial historical significance which all ends up going under the hammer. With worldwide interest in these objects, will the Flog It! record for the highest value item sold at auction be broken when the sale goes on to raise thousands of pounds? Paul also gets the chance to explore Longleat House, learning more about its controversial creator and wonderful Elizabethan architecture.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: Returning to England from Istanbul on the Orient Express, Hercule Poirot is asked to investigate a murder.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys criss-crossed by clear rivers. Its western border adjoins Kielder Forest, one of the largest man-made forests in Europe. This vast natural expanse is a haven for numerous bird species, including the osprey, once extinct in the UK but now returning annually from Africa to breed. The park is also rich in history, including extensive stretches of Hadrian’s Wall.

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC
programme.desc: DJ Spoony takes a musical journey that showcases the artists, songs and genres that have done so much to shape the nation’s musical landscape over the years – from old school right up to the present. Spoony has personally selected a playlist of 40 great tracks that bring together some of the most inspiring names from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage - and finds the likes of Seal, The Selecter and Soul II Soul rubbing shoulders with FKA Twigs, Miss Dynamite and Dame Shirley Bassey. Spoony also celebrates the rich vein of collaboration that runs through so much Black British music, sharing songs and performances from acts, including Wretch 32 and Shola Ama, Loyle Carner and Jorja Smith, Little Simz and Cleo Sol, and Tinie Tempah and Labrinth.

programme.title: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: Alan Black introduces Joan Armatrading in concert at the Hammersmith Odeon in 1977.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from day 8 of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon is obsessed with the love story of how her parents met - all she wants is her own romance to sweep her off her feet. But finding love is tough when you’re the only daughter of a notorious crime family. When Shannon meets Arran, she thinks her luck has finally changed. She feels the spark. But will her family allow it when they realise who he is?

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartney’s original Höfner bass over 50 years ago is one of rock ‘n’ roll’s great mysteries. This is the story of the iconic instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring exclusive interviews with Paul, his brother Mike, Klaus Voormann, Elvis Costello, and the roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith are in the Peak District National Park, famed for its breathtaking landscapes and wildlife. But despite its wild and rugged appearance, there’s a huge amount of work needed to protect it. John spends the day as a ranger and meets volunteers who have devoted years to caring for this landscape. Charlotte jumps on a bike to explore an accessible cycling route and heads out across the moors with a scientist monitoring one of the park's most elusive residents – the mountain hare. Adam Henson is also in the Peaks to see what happens when farmers stop going it alone and start working together.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 9 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi is in Bristol to help Jasper Thompson, a local man whose pioneering homeless project transforms shipping containers into temporary accommodation. He wants to raise awareness about his new community cafe, so who better than Andi to help him put on an unforgettable launch party with a Caribbean flavour? To entice locals to the event and kick off the cafe with a bang, Andi must put together a unique and delicious feast that draws on her and Jasper’s own roots in Antigua and Jamaica while also paying tribute to Bristol’s wider Caribbean heritage, using only the best local produce. The cafe itself needs a complete refurb, but can Jasper get it ready in time, and can Andi harness the Bristolians' unique creative spirit to make sure the event’s a roaring success?

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the London Marathon continues as the 59,000 runners begin to reach the end of their 26.2 mile journey across the capital, each of them with their own unique reason for taking part in this year’s event. Gabby Logan and a team of reporters are with them every step of the way as they reveal the uplifting and inspiring stories of many of the charity runners taking part.

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Coverage of Arsenal v Lyon in the 2026 Women's Champions League.

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan has the best of the day’s action from the London Marathon. Four elite champions are crowned alongside 59,000 inspirational charity runners, making this the biggest edition of the event ever staged and raising millions for some good causes.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The Williamsons go for glory at their local show on Lewis, where their best sheep compete with the island's cream of the crop. Lucy and Adam reignite their dairy dreams, but their new baby arrives just as the workload ramps up. And Callum and Zara head south for a farm safety awareness event to commemorate those farmers and their families affected by accidents at work.

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to one of the most spectacular countries in the world - Colombia. For 50 years, Colombia has been in the grip of a brutal civil war that has killed more than 200,000 people and displaced seven million. But in late 2016, a peace deal was signed promising to end the conflict and finally bring peace to the country. In this hour-long documentary for the award-winning This World strand, Simon explores Colombia at a pivotal point in its history. He travels into the jungle and comes face to face with the guerrilla army Farc, which is now promising to lay down arms. In the Pacific coast city of Buenaventura, Simon finds out more about the fearsome right-wing paramilitary gangs who now dominate the cocaine trade. As the Farc abandon the countryside, there is a fear that these groups will only grow in power. Travelling in the countryside, Simon meets the coca farmers who are demanding government support to stop growing coca and stop the flow of money to criminal gangs. With land ownership, poverty and drugs at the heart of Colombia's problems, it is in the countryside that the country's precarious future will be decided.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: At midday on a busy high street in Birmingham, a group of armed robbers drive a truck into a jewellery shop, stealing £300,000 worth of gold. They escape in less than 60 seconds. Forensic scene investigator Drew Birtles is dispatched to the jewellery shop to recover any forensic clues which could help identify the robbers. The investigation becomes more challenging when no real forensic evidence is found at the crime scene and the robbers switch cars - leaving the trail to go cold. Suspects are identified, and investigators hope that forensic evidence from the cars could provide the vital information needed to help solve the case and bring down the thieves.

programme.title: The Untouchables
programme.desc: Chicago, 1920s: During the Prohibition era, federal agent Eliot Ness assembles a small, hand-picked team to bring down notorious gangster Al Capone. Award-winning film starring Sean Connery.

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever. He kick-starts with Gigantoraptor, an eight-metre-tall giant that laid the biggest eggs in history. Then, courtesy of a monster truck crash, he meets the miniscule Magyarosaurus. But that's not freaky enough, so finally he focuses on a dinosaur that head-butted its rivals to death - something Steve's hoping to replicate with the mother of all robots. Weird!

programme.desc: Following his selection for the GB squad, artistic gymnast Winston is heading off to Germany with his teammates for an international competition where they will be up against some of the world’s major players. Even though his team are there to support him, he has to face his fear and take on the pommel, the apparatus that has caused him more than his fair share of problems during the last season. The boys also face the distraction of a trip to an out-of-this-world car museum - but will Winston be able to hold it together when the team comes up against some of the world’s best gymnasts from the USA and France? Back in Birmingham, the display girls get to grips with a new routine in preparation for a performance in the lead-up to the Commonwealth Games. programme.title: Daredevil

programme.title: Titane
